summary: Read-only QA accepts the M7 systemic interaction recovery planning package after one request-changes round. The task graph is bounded, content lock remains blocked, no product code scope is included, and current-state files now consistently route to M7-SYSTEMIC-INTERACTION-RECOVERY-001 with no active waiting Human Gate.

PASS: no output; no implementation task is accidentally READY before planning integration.

tests_run.result: PASS: no active stale Human Gate strings remain as current state.

decisions.decision: Accept the recovery task graph sequence: M7-SYSTEMIC-INTERACTION-RECOVERY-001 -> M7-CORE-ACTION-LOOP-UX-001 -> M7-MAP-DECISION-SURFACE-SYNC-001 -> M7-SYSTEMIC-INTERACTION-VALIDATION-001.
M7 content lock remains BLOCKED with content_lock_accepted=false and human_gate_state=REQUEST_CHANGES_SYSTEMIC_INTERACTION_RECOVERY_REQUIRED.
The product owner has already returned REQUEST_CHANGES, so repair work can proceed; no active waiting Human Gate exists until recovery validation returns content lock for reconsideration.

requested_action: Integrate the accepted planning package, close M7-SYSTEMIC-INTERACTION-RECOVERY-001 after main integration, then start M7-CORE-ACTION-LOOP-UX-001.
